titleStmt.title: Letter from the Administrative Area Officer to Malachy J. Kelly, 24 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Administrative Area Officer, Royal Hospital Dublin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from the Administrative Area Officer to Malachy Kelly (1850-1916), the Chief Crown Solicitor. The letter concerns the injury of Patrick Sheehan, a worker for the District Purchasing Officer in Cork. It accounts that Sheehan dislocated his ankle while carrying sacks. Mr Sheehan believes he is entitled to compensation.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3785

correspAction.persName: Administrative Area Officer, Royal Hospital Dublin

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Ref No.155/55746.
Subject. Injury to Patrick Sheehan. A.A.O., Ireland,
Royal Hospital,
Dublin.
Tel. No. 4809.
24th February, 1916.
The Chief Crown Solictor,
Dublin Castle
Sir, Herewith I enclose corres-
pondance concerning an accident to a work-
man( named Patrick Sheehan) employed by
the District Purchasing Officer, Cork. He
dislocated his ankle whilst engaged in
carrying a bundle of empty sacks. I understand that the Government
do not insure against accidents, and would
be obliged if you would kindly inform me what
the position is with regard to this man, who
considers that he is entitled to compensation. Yours faithfully,
Enniduich
Colonel,
Administrative Area Officer.
M.R.
Administrative Area Officer.
What were the in this main
of the were in Waiting please it
M. Kelly
25.2.16
